title: epacris ciliata
intro: Epacris ciliata, also known as the Red Stringybark or Red Heath, is a species of flowering plant in the family Epacridaceae. It is native to eastern Australia, where it grows as a shrub in heathlands and woodlands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, with its leaves and stems having antimicrobial properties that can help to treat wounds, skin infections, and colds. Epacris ciliata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been used to promote respiratory health, soothe sore throats, and reduce inflammation.
